item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be fair, the entry does state that it doesn't respond to further attempts at communication, and the only entry from the foundation that we see is the first one, the one it actually responded to. We know from the text that the foundation tried more after that first attempt without success, but it can be reasonably assumed that all the attempts at communication don't make it to the file if they do not elicit a response.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 because it reminds me of Lotso from Toy Story 3. We're all trash waiting to be thrown away.
One thing bothered me.
I'm surprised the foundation doesn't attempt to communicate with the Diary again especially after the Diary claims to know where to find Eric because it knows his secrets. Now that the Diary feels betrayed by Eric, couldn't the researchers manipulate those feelings to get the Diary to spill Eric's secrets.
Adding an addendum that tells the reader that the foundation plans to attempt future interrogations would make sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ooo, I love this! The progression was absolutely excellent, and something about the dialogue (which I, unfortunately, can't put my finger on) was just so well-done.

Also, 5102 sort of practising making human body parts with its pages before finally creating a whole damn person out of paper to come and get its revenge on Eric was such a nice touch. I didn't put it together 'til the end, but it immediately clicked in hindsight.

Overall, this was beautifully done, and I honestly can't think of anything that jumps out at me as being badly-done. Certainly a +1 from me c:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gave me chills, makes me feel sorry for Eric

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read it as the diary being both sides of that conversation. It's clearly a little crazy by that point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Speaking as someone who has read most of the "Eric" SCP's on this site, this is very good. It makes sense, atleast in the sense for a young child to have a diary, and the personality of the diary is a also understandable.

However, the seeming cruelty of "Eric"(If that is even them and not the researchers trying to get a reaction out of the diary), seems really out of character during that discussion near the end.

Either way, +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hot Damn, that took a real twist. I loved it!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh jeez. First I smiled, thinking this was going to be one of the wholesome ones. Then I got my heart broken. And at the end, really concerned. Dear Diary needs a hug, please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the Eric mentioned here meant to be the PoI “Eric”?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved your prose, and the descent into madness is paced perfectly. This probably would resonate better with me as a Tale than as a skip, though. It's a sad, abandoned, angry diary and that's about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Long time lurker since 2013 here, but jeez I haven't been terrified by a skip in a really long time, and this really gets to me. The skip itself is pretty mundane, but the dialogue's incredibly menacing tone earns a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Coming from someone who’ve witness someone’s psycho ex or people with these type of tendencies, this dialogue is awfully realistic.

+1

Creepy, terrifying, concise, effective.

Honestly, at this point, that could be your subtitle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Been writing an Eric skip these past few days and let me tell you, I’m in love w/ this!! Eric’s a bad friend.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Henzoid, Justice and ratking for their always valuable feedback!
